text: ​ @FinnOtaku  I understand that the development has been delayed?
author: @fleksoytpmvs

text: @fleksoytpmvs  While I was still drafting H-Side, I got roped into a collab and ended up doing some stuff I quite frankly didn't sign up for, though ended up sticking with it out of courtesy. Turns out forcing myself to do that burned out the motivation I was saving for the arranging phase of H-Side and even to my surprise I've yet to really replenish it. Almost 9 months and the arrangement is not even remotely close to being finished.

author: @madotsuki417

text:
I'm afraid to ask this but: will this be the last of the _-Side Medleys? 
My reasoning is: musically, there are 7 major keys (A-G). Since this is G-side, that should theoretically mean that this will be the last mix. That being said, since B-Side was an April fools medley, maybe there will be one more, but I'm not overly optimistic.
author: @iaport

text:
Okay, it looks like there's a bit of a misconception regarding the name of this series ^__^;
First of all, the "Side" bit of the name is supposed to be a nod towards vinyl records and cassette tapes that tend to have an a-side and a b-side. Which makes all of the titles from C-Side onward nonsensical by default, so no need to worry about there being some arbitrary restrictions. There is gonna be a H-Side of YTPMV eventually and no amount of piano keys is gonna change that 😄
author: @FinnOtaku

text: I thought so too 😅
author: @Atlas_741

text: This is story, man I'm happy that you're well
author: @TagFul19

text:
Never thought I would make it in one of these, I feel very very honored! Once again coming with a fantastic medley :D
Don't know if it's just me, but the medleys you do kind of feel like a Rewind for YTPMV, which in a sense is very fantastic and brings me the joy it did back in the day with the first YouTube Rewinds.
author: @RUR2D2

text: The Rewind-ness is somewhat intentional. After I began showing the release dates and such I've tried to balance between the most recent fads and some of the older videos as much as possible. To showcase some of the history of YTPMV in a sense
author: @FinnOtaku

text: That's what I absolutely love about these, because this is a big showcase of YTPMV history, and in general for me it hits me with a lot of joy and nostalgia to see and hear vids and bgms that have been made and used throughout YTPMV history, the legacy that it has left and just in general how much progress there has been made, and it makes me realize just how some things just never really die, specially in this community
author: @RUR2D2
